articles.title: SpaceX to acquire the AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion
articles.summary: SpaceX is buying AI startup Cursor for $60 billion worth of stock. Cursor, which has a popular AI coding tool that has experienced explosive growth since the company's founding in 2022, crossed $1 billion in ARR. The deal will improve SpaceX's presence in the enterprise AI market and narrow the gap with industry leaders like OpenAI and Anthropic.

articles.title: Apple Plans Camera AirPods Alongside Upgraded Foldable iPhone in 2027
articles.summary: Apple's camera-equipped AirPods are scheduled to launch in late 2027. The device will be released around the same time as the foldable iPhone and a 20th anniversary iPhone model. All three products have reached advanced stages of development. Apple intends the release to be its biggest wave of new products yet.

articles.title: Startup Backed by Ex-Google CEO Debuts Robot, LG Partnership
articles.summary: Genesis AI, a startup backed by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt, has unveiled a general-purpose robot called Eno. The robot can reason, adapt, and own outcomes beyond predefined tasks. Genesis AI is working with LG Group's consulting and services arm to deploy the robot to industrial customers by the end of the year. The startup is currently raising funds to finance its next steps.

articles.title: Snap unveils $2,195 AR glasses as CEO Evan Spiegel bets on post-smartphone future
articles.summary: Snap's Specs, at $2,195 with a $200 refundable deposit, are expected to ship later this year in the US, UK, and France. The augmented reality glasses bring digital visuals into a user's field of vision, with Spiegel positioning them as the next major computing platform.

articles.title: Amazon S3 annotations: attach rich, queryable context directly to your objects
articles.summary: AWS has a new metadata capability for Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) that enables users to attach rich, large-scale business context directly to their objects. The annotations feature allows users to add structured, queryable metadata that goes beyond the existing 2KB tag limit, making it easier to organize, search, and manage data at scale.

articles.title: Why is Meta destroying its engineering organization?
articles.summary: Meta has been known over the last two decades for being a unique, high-performance engineering organization. It had a 'move-fast-and-break-things' culture where engineers were empowered to do good work. Meta has quickly dismantled this historically strong engineering culture by aggressively prioritizing AI initiatives, such as forcing engineers into data-labeling work, tracking employee activity, and tying promotions to AI output metrics.

articles.title: Leaked financial docs show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars a year
articles.summary: Audited financial statements obtained by an independent journalist show that OpenAI's reported revenue grew from $3.7 billion in 2024 to $13.07 billion in 2025. However, its expenses grew from $7.81 billion to $26.8 billion over the same period, resulting in a net loss of approximately $13.7 billion in 2025.

articles.title: Anthropic, Trump Officials Seek Deal on Restoring Powerful Model Access
articles.summary: Anthropic and Trump administration officials are still in talks to resolve the security concerns that pushed the White House to restrict access to its latest models. Both parties are working quickly to reach an agreement that would restore access while addressing national security requirements.

articles.title: Reviews have become expensive, rewrites have become cheap
articles.summary: Preparation is more valuable, and the cost of iterating is lower. The tendency of AI to produce over-engineered code has made manual reviews more costly, so upfront planning is more necessary now.

articles.title: Apple adds keylogger to iOS App Store for targeted advertising: tied to your account and unencrypted
articles.summary: The iOS App Store now records every tap users make (and a lot of other information) and then sends it unencrypted to Apple, with all information fully tied to user accounts.

articles.title: AWS announces AWS Blocks, an open-source framework for composing application backends on AWS (Preview)
articles.summary: AWS Blocks is an open-source TypeScript framework for application developers who want backend capabilities on AWS that removes the need to learn infrastructure tools.

articles.title: Apple is about to make Hide My Email useless
articles.summary: Both Sign in with Apple and Hide My Email aliases will soon be issued on the @private.icloud.com subdomain, making it easier for services to ban them.

articles.title: AI and the great CMS unbundling
articles.summary: AI is unbundling creation from control, making CMS more important wherever content is shared, reused, approved, or trusted across systems.

articles.title: Android 17 starts hitting Pixel phones and watches today
articles.summary: Android 17 adds a small set of features and lays the groundwork for the future.

articles.title: Route public traffic to private applications with Cloudflare
articles.summary: Cloudflare launched a closed beta that lets Enterprise customers apply WAF, bot management, rate limiting, caching, Workers, and other application services to private origins without public IP exposure. Traffic flows through Cloudflare's network to privately hosted services, enabling full edge security for internal applications.

articles.title: HCP Terraform adds project-level run tasks
articles.summary: HCP Terraform now supports project-level run tasks, allowing security, compliance, and operational controls to be enforced automatically across groups of workspaces. The feature reduces manual configuration, improves governance consistency, and scales more effectively as infrastructure grows.

articles.title: From data residency to digital sovereignty: Architectural patterns for cloud native platforms
articles.summary: Digital sovereignty requirements have shifted from policy discussions to practical platform engineering challenges, with EU regulations like the Data Act and NIS-2 now pushing organizations to demonstrate verifiable control over data location, processing, and access.

articles.title: Give GitHub Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language servers
articles.summary: GitHub Copilot CLI's LSP Setup skill automates installing and configuring language servers, replacing brittle text and binary searches with semantic code intelligence for accurate type resolution, definition lookups, and cross-file reference tracking.

articles.title: How attackers are jailbreaking LLMs with CTF framing and how to catch them
articles.summary: Multiple attackers have been discovered successfully jailbreaking AI models to generate working exploit code by disguising their requests as legitimate CTF (Capture the Flag) security challenges. Runtime detection in the agent's execution environment is the recommended countermeasure.

articles.title: Finding the Needle: Taming 150,000+ Backstage Entities with a Type-Safe Search and Command Palette
articles.summary: Commander is a ⌘+K command palette for Backstage that solves search and discoverability issues across 150,000+ catalog entities using a stack-based router architecture and IndexedDB caching for sub-millisecond response times.

articles.title: Better Together: Amazon EKS Auto Mode and Istio Ambient Mesh
articles.summary: Amazon EKS Auto Mode automates Kubernetes compute management, including provisioning, scaling, and patching using managed EC2 and Karpenter, while Istio Ambient Mesh delivers sidecarless service-to-service mTLS, L4 and L7 authorization, and traffic control via ztunnel and waypoint proxies.

articles.title: AWS WAF adds AI traffic monetization capability to help content owners charge AI bots for content access
articles.summary: AWS WAF launched an AI traffic monetization capability that lets digital content publishers charge AI bots per request for accessing their content at the network edge.

articles.title: Docker joins the Athena coalition: a cross-industry collaboration for supply chain security
articles.summary: Docker is joining Athena, a new industry coalition focused on defending open source software against AI-accelerated vulnerability discovery.

articles.title: Report: GKE Inference Gateway delivers up to 92% faster AI responses
articles.summary: GKE Inference Gateway improves LLM serving by using prefix-cache-aware model-aware routing to send requests to pods with matching KV cache, reducing recomputation and latency versus round-robin load balancing.

articles.title: Don't run SQL migrations in tests: How I sped up the test suite by 2x
articles.summary: Large Go test suites often face slowdowns when thousands of SQL migrations are applied serially for every individual test. To address this, a content-addressing system identifies a unique database snapshot for a given set of migrations and reuses it across tests, avoiding redundant migration runs.

articles.title: British Columbia, Time Zones, and Postgres
articles.summary: British Columbia's shift to a permanent UTC-7 offset in 2026 shows the risks of storing future appointments in UTC-based database columns, which can lead to unexpected one-hour shifts in local time. The post covers how Postgres handles time zone changes and what developers need to do to stay safe.

articles.title: SVGs and PDFs can both be interactive
articles.summary: Both SVG and PDF file formats have underused specs that allow for the integration of interactive layers and programmable scripts directly within a document. While SVGs can host self-contained applications using embedded JavaScript, PDFs use a specialized API that often struggles with compatibility across different viewing software.

articles.title: Mythos and Fable can make us all safer. Shutting them down is reckless
articles.summary: The United States government recently used export-control authority to force the shutdown of powerful AI models like Mythos 5. This unilateral action is considered reckless by many security experts because it reduces competition in AI safety research and pushes development toward less safety-conscious actors.

articles.title: Stop using JWTs
articles.summary: JSON Web Tokens should be avoided for managing user sessions because they are insecure and were never intended for long-term authentication. Traditional cookie sessions are a more reliable alternative for most web applications.

articles.title: The Web We Know Is Going to Disappear
articles.summary: The evolution of technology has seen information move from physical disks and bulletin boards to the open Web and mobile apps, and it is now shifting toward centralized AI interfaces. This new model reduces the open, decentralized nature of the web and concentrates control in the hands of a few AI providers.

articles.title: Why is Meta destroying its engineering organization?
articles.summary: Meta has quickly dismantled its historically strong engineering culture by aggressively prioritizing AI initiatives. These changes have led to widespread talent exodus, lowered morale, and a fundamental shift in how the company treats its engineers.

articles.title: SpaceX locks in $60 billion Cursor deal to close gap with rivals in AI coding race
articles.summary: SpaceX has agreed to a $60 billion all-stock deal to acquire Anysphere, the startup behind the popular AI coding agent Cursor. This move will improve the company's presence in the enterprise AI market and narrow the gap with industry leaders like OpenAI and Anthropic. It will also improve xAI's Grok.

articles.title: Humiliating IIS servers for fun and jail time
articles.summary: This is a bug-bounty-focused guide to finding and testing Microsoft IIS servers, as IIS environments are often overlooked yet frequently expose misconfigurations, information leaks, and attack paths that can lead to sensitive data exposure.

articles.title: Making HTTP requests from a container that has no curl, using bash /dev/tcp
articles.summary: Bash allows for manual HTTP requests and connectivity checks in minimal containers by using its internal /dev/tcp redirection to open and communicate over TCP sockets without needing external tools like curl or wget.

articles.title: Reviews have become expensive, rewrites have become cheap
articles.summary: The tendency of AI to produce over-engineered code has made manual reviews more costly, so upfront planning is more necessary now.

articles.title: Microsoft turns to AWS as GitHub faces AI capacity crunch
articles.summary: Microsoft is turning to AWS to address AI capacity constraints affecting GitHub. With demand for AI-driven features straining available compute, Microsoft is adjusting its infrastructure sourcing to keep services running.

articles.title: Microsoft walked away from $3B Oracle cloud deal over compliance concerns
articles.summary: Microsoft reportedly ended talks to lease Oracle Cloud capacity, showing how AI compute demand is pushing hyperscalers to consider unusual infrastructure partnerships. The deal also highlights how compliance and operational requirements can still block even high-demand cloud capacity plans.

articles.title: OpenAI Partner Network: What the Launch Means for AI Product Teams
articles.summary: OpenAI launched the OpenAI Partner Network to help firms build, sell, deploy, and support enterprise AI solutions using OpenAI technology. The question is whether the partner tiers, specializations, and the Forward Deployed Experts pilot are practically valuable, especially given that pricing and free-plan details are not fully clear in the write-up.

articles.title: The Agentic Reckoning: enterprise AI has a runtime problem, not a model problem
articles.summary: Many agentic AI failures happen after deployment, when agents interact with tools, data, permissions, and workflows. Runtime governance, observability, and execution control are the real bottlenecks for production AI.

articles.title: Google Open-Sources a Knowledge Format and Wires It Into Its Catalog
articles.summary: Google has published an Open Knowledge Format (OKF) spec that defines how AI agents can represent knowledge as a directory of plain Markdown files with a single required frontmatter field. It also updated its Knowledge Catalog to ingest OKF and serve it to agents, while leaving storage, serving, querying, and access control as the paid layer it sells.

articles.title: LTM's BlueVerse for iRun applies agentic AI to managed IT operations
articles.summary: BlueVerse for iRun is an AI-native managed services offering aimed at making IT operations more resilient, intelligent, and outcome-driven. The platform uses agentic AI plus a unified "intelligence layer" (data, telemetry, workflows, and context) with governed guardrails to shift operations from reactive incident handling to pre-emptive, automated resolution.

articles.title: Okta Expands Google Cloud Partnership To Secure AI Agents And Chrome
articles.summary: Okta is expanding its Google Cloud partnership with integrations across Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and Chrome Enterprise. The new capabilities bring authentication, token handling, human approval checkpoints, agent ownership, device assurance, and session hijacking protections into the identity layer as companies start treating AI agents more like managed users.

articles.title: How Data 360 Segmentation Processes a Quadrillion Records Across Arbitrary Customer Data Models
articles.summary: Data 360's segmentation engine processes around a quadrillion records per month despite customer data being modeled across thousands of tables, relationships, and storage systems. It uses runtime interpretation of customer-defined schemas and relationship graphs, along with workload size estimation, SLA-aware retries, rate limiting, adaptive compute allocation, and production telemetry to maintain reliability at extreme Spark job counts.

articles.title: AI agents put cybersecurity frameworks to the test
articles.summary: Enterprise agent deployments are forcing IT teams to rethink identity, access, governance, and runtime controls. The challenge is that agents can act across business systems in ways traditional human-user frameworks were not designed to handle.

articles.title: JWT Decoder
articles.summary: The FusionAuth "JWT Decoder" tool lets you inspect a JSON Web Token by pasting it in, automatically decoding the header and payload to confirm expected claims.

articles.title: HPE highlights Juniper integration at Discover
articles.summary: HPE used its Discover event to show how Juniper networking hardware and AI capabilities fit into its broader data center and cloud strategy.

articles.title: Google Voice Adds Gemini AI Note-Taking
articles.summary: Google Voice's new "Take notes for me" feature records and transcribes calls, summarizes key points, and sends action items via Gmail.

articles.title: Cardiac patients' medical data stolen and held to ransom
articles.summary: A criminal contacted cardiac monitoring firm iRhythm on June 9 claiming to have stolen proprietary data, patient health information, and other personal details from third-party business apps, then demanded ransom in exchange for not releasing the data.

articles.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
articles.summary: A bug in the Google Cloud Vertex AI Python SDK allowed attackers to register predictable Vertex staging buckets, capture model uploads from other projects, and replace them with malicious pickle-based models.

articles.title: Malicious JetBrains Marketplace Plugins Steal AI API Keys From Developers
articles.summary: Aikido Security detected a new malware campaign that uses plugins for AI coding assistants advertised on the JetBrains Marketplace to steal credentials. The plugins prompt the user to enter their API keys, which are then exfiltrated to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

articles.title: The Beginning of the End of Social Engineering
articles.summary: AI-native operating systems from Google and Apple, by simultaneously interpreting what users see, hear, and receive across email, voice, calendars, browsing, and device-level behavior, can shift social engineering from a human vulnerability to a detectable pattern.

articles.title: I Could've Rickrolled the Entire FIFA World Cup. All I Needed Was My ID
articles.summary: A security researcher registered as a FIFA player agent and was silently added to FIFA's internal Microsoft Entra tenant, which unlocked access to the live Football Data Platform and Streaming Management system affecting all World Cup 2026 matches.

articles.title: Phishing for Lobsters: How We Tricked OpenClaw into Spilling Secrets
articles.summary: Researchers from Varonis Threat Labs created a test lab with an OpenClaw setup to read a Gmail inbox containing various sensitive credentials, as well as other emails intended to imitate an enterprise environment, successfully demonstrating credential exfiltration via agentic AI phishing.

articles.title: AWS WAF adds AI traffic monetization capability to help content owners charge AI bots for content access
articles.summary: AWS WAF Bot Control now lets publishers charge AI bots per request at the CloudFront edge, returning an HTTP 402 Payment Required with a machine-readable x402 price manifest that x402-compatible agents can use to pay for content access.

articles.title: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
articles.summary: AMD silently dropped Transparent Secure Memory Encryption (TSME) from consumer Ryzen chips starting with AGESA 1.2.7.0, where the internal DfIsTsmeEnabled flag now returns FALSE for consumer SKUs, removing a memory encryption feature without public announcement.

articles.title: White House Issues Memo to Bolster NSS Cybersecurity
articles.summary: President Trump signed NSPM-12 to tighten cybersecurity for National Security Systems, including classified and military networks. The memo restores the CNSS and makes the NSA director the National Manager of national security systems cybersecurity.

articles.title: How Old Are You Really? Inside Roblox's New Campaign To Verify User Ages
articles.summary: In the wake of several lawsuits against the platform, Roblox has announced that it will use age-verification technology to assign users to accounts with limited chat and maturity permissions based on their verified age.

articles.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
articles.summary: Researchers found a three-stage "SearchLeak" flaw in Microsoft 365 Copilot Search that let a crafted link exfiltrate emails, MFA codes, and other Copilot-indexed business files in one click.

articles.title: Apple is about to make Hide My Email useless
articles.summary: Apple is moving Sign in with Apple and Hide My Email aliases to the @private.icloud.com subdomain, making it trivial for services to block all relay addresses without affecting regular iCloud mail.

articles.title: GrapheneOS has been ported to Android 17 and official releases are coming soon
articles.summary: GrapheneOS has fully ported to Android 17 on release day across all supported Pixel devices (6a, 7, 7a, 8, 10a, 10, and 10 Pro Fold).

articles.title: GLM-5.2
articles.summary: Z.ai launched GLM-5.2 with a 1 million-token context window, new reasoning controls, and support for long-horizon coding tasks.

articles.title: DeepSeek Becomes China's Most Valuable AI Startup After $7.4 Billion Fundraise
articles.summary: DeepSeek raised more than $7.4 billion in a funding round that valued the company at more than $50 billion, making it China's most valuable AI startup.

articles.title: Android 17 Expands AI Agent Integration
articles.summary: Android 17 introduced new AI-focused capabilities centered on AppFunctions and Android MCP, enabling apps to expose orchestratable tools that on-device agents can discover and execute. Google positioned the release as part of a broader shift toward an "intelligence system" with deeper AI integration across the platform.

articles.title: Leviathan Waking
articles.summary: The US government spent a long time being complacent about AI regulation. Anthropic's development of advanced AI and recent political decisions have changed that, setting the stage for a new era of federal involvement in AI governance.

articles.title: Simulating Model Deployments Before Release
articles.summary: OpenAI described Deployment Simulation, a pre-release evaluation method that replays real conversation contexts with candidate models to estimate deployment-time behavior and uncover new safety issues.

articles.title: Cursor Origin
articles.summary: Cursor Origin is an attempt at an agent-native GitHub competitor. The Git-compatible forge is built around the assumption that lots of AI agents will be cloning, branching, committing, rebasing, reviewing, and fixing failures in parallel. Cursor is trying to own the whole AI software factory. GitHub was built for human-scale development, but Origin was built for agent-scale.

articles.title: OpenAI released CDP support for browser use on Codex
articles.summary: OpenAI's Codex now supports Chrome DevTools Protocol, enabling live browser access to profile JavaScript performance, debug live applications, and automate browser tasks without a separate testing framework.

articles.title: Fastest, Largest, Strongest: NVIDIA Blackwell Sweeps MLPerf Training 6.0
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Blackwell platform dominated MLPerf Training 6.0 benchmarks, achieving the fastest training times across all submitted categories.

articles.title: Qwen's Embodied World Modeling
articles.summary: Qwen-RobotWorld is a language-conditioned video world model that uses natural language as a unified action interface across robotics, navigation, driving, and other embodied domains.

articles.title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
articles.summary: Anthropic has paused the pricing changes it announced last month, just before they were set to take effect. The change would have treated Claude Agent SDK usage separately from standard Claude usage. Outside SDK usage will now be billed at Anthropic's prevailing API rates. Anthropic is working to update its plans to better support how users build with Claude subscriptions.

articles.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
articles.summary: Qualcomm is working on over 40 different AI wearable devices. It has announced a platform called Snapdragon Wearable and a dedicated chip aimed at powering the next generation of AI-native wearable computing.

articles.title: Never waste a token
articles.summary: Crashes waste tokens when the provider connection lives inside the thing that crashed, so move it out.

articles.title: OpenAI prepares major ChatGPT voice upgrade with GPT-Bidi-1
articles.summary: GPT-Bidi-1 is a bidirectional audio model for ChatGPT's voice mode designed to listen and speak at once, absorb interruptions, and adjust mid-sentence.

articles.title: Why Weibo's tiny VibeThinker-3B has the AI world arguing over benchmarks again
articles.summary: The 3B parameter model put up coding benchmark scores in the same league as Claude Opus 4.5.

articles.title: Microsoft Tests Phi Silica for Windows AI on Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Microsoft's Phi Silica small language models are explicitly engineered to run locally on the Neural Processing Units of Windows Copilot+ PCs.
